info.title: Open Opus API
info.description: Open Opus — an open, curated catalogue of classical composers and their works. Free, no authentication. Used by the impromptu pack as the canonical source for work facts: it resolves a free-text query (composer + work, e.g. 'Beethoven Symphony 5') into a stable work id, title, composer, and genre, so a `Work` node can canonicalize onto the Open Opus spine and dedupe against works the user has already rated.

get.summary: Free-text search across composers, works and genres.
get.description: Searches the whole catalogue with a single free-text `query` (e.g. 'beethoven symphony 5', 'chopin nocturne op 9'). Returns a `results` array ordered by relevance; each element carries a `composer` object and — for work hits — a `work` object. Composer-only hits set `work` to null, so to resolve a specific work take the FIRST result whose `work.id` is non-null. Use a fairly specific query (composer surname + form + number) for a clean top hit.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: URL-encoded free-text search, e.g. 'beethoven%20symphony%205'. Include the composer surname to disambiguate common forms ('symphony no 5' alone is ambiguous across composers).

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Zero-based paging offset. Pass 0 for the first (most relevant) page of results.

200.description: Search results. `status.success` is 'true' on a well-formed response even when `results` is empty.

OmnisearchHit.description: One search hit: always a composer, plus a work when the hit is a work.

work.description: The matched work, or null for a composer-only hit.

id.description: Stable Open Opus work id. Use as Work.workId when persisting.

title.description: Work title, e.g. 'Symphony no. 5 in C minor, op. 67'.

subtitle.description: Nickname or subtitle, often empty, e.g. 'Emperor'.

popular.description: '1' if a well-known / frequently performed work.
